testcase.name: test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set

failure.message: ExceptionGroup: Hypothesis found 2 distinct failures. (2 sub-exceptions)

testcase.failure: + Exception Group Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py", line 111, in test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set | products=st.lists(product_record_strategy(), min_size=1, max_size=10, unique_by=lambda p: p["product_id"]), | ^^^^^^^ | File "/opt/hostedtoolcache/Python/3.11.15/x64/lib/python3.11/site-packages/hypothesis/core.py", line 2264, in wrapped_test | raise the_error_hypothesis_found | ExceptionGroup: Hypothesis found 2 distinct failures. (2 sub-exceptions) +-+---------------- 1 ---------------- | Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py", line 196, in test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set | assert "tenant" not in item, ( | AssertionError: Product prod_0000: 'tenant' field should be removed | assert 'tenant' not in {'product_id': 'prod_0000', 'name': 'Product prod_0000', 'is_parent': True, 'active': True, ...} | Falsifying example: test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set( | self=<tests.unit.test_property_channel_transform.TestProperty4ChannelToEventIdTransformation object at 0x7f16713bb690>, | products=[{'product_id': 'prod_0000', | 'name': 'Product prod_0000', | 'is_parent': True, | 'active': True, | 'tenant': 'h-dcn'}], | carts=[], | ) | Explanation: | These lines were always and only run by failing examples: | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py:61 | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py:62 | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py:193 | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/scripts/migrate_products.py:463 | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/scripts/migrate_products.py:469 | /opt/hostedtoolcache/Python/3.11.15/x64/lib/python3.11/logging/__init__.py:1501 | | You can reproduce this example by temporarily adding @reproduce_failure('6.155.2', b'AXicY2R0ZGg0QCMYHJmBNAMDAEbiBFQ=') as a decorator on your test case +---------------- 2 ---------------- | Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py", line 193, in test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set | assert "channel" not in item, ( | AssertionError: Product prod_0000: 'channel' field should be removed | assert 'channel' not in {'product_id': 'prod_0000', 'name': 'Product prod_0000', 'is_parent': True, 'active': True, ...} | Falsifying example: test_channel_removed_and_event_id_set( | # The test always failed when commented parts were varied together. | self=<tests.unit.test_property_channel_transform.TestProperty4ChannelToEventIdTransformation object at 0x7f16713bb690>, | products=[{'product_id': 'prod_0000', | 'name': 'Product prod_0000', | 'is_parent': True, | 'active': True, | 'channel': 'h-dcn'}], | carts=[], # or any other generated value | ) | Explanation: | These lines were always and only run by failing examples: | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py:59 | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_property_channel_transform.py:193 | testcase.name: test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly

failure.message: ExceptionGroup: Hypothesis found 2 distinct failures. (2 sub-exceptions)

testcase.failure: + Exception Group Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_user_group_config_property.py", line 90, in test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly | @settings(max_examples=100) | ^^^^^^^ | File "/opt/hostedtoolcache/Python/3.11.15/x64/lib/python3.11/site-packages/hypothesis/core.py", line 2264, in wrapped_test | raise the_error_hypothesis_found | ExceptionGroup: Hypothesis found 2 distinct failures. (2 sub-exceptions) +-+---------------- 1 ---------------- | Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_user_group_config_property.py", line 100, in test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly | expected_groups = EXPECTED_GROUPS[username] | ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~^^^^^^^^^^ | KeyError: 'test-event-participant' | Falsifying example: test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly( | self=<tests.unit.test_user_group_config_property.TestProperty5TestUserGroupConfigurationCorrectness object at 0x7f1670271990>, | user={'username': 'test-event-participant', | 'email': 'pjageers+testpresmeet@gmail.com', | 'groups': ['event_participant', 'hdcnLeden']}, | ) | | You can reproduce this example by temporarily adding @reproduce_failure('6.155.2', b'AEED') as a decorator on your test case +---------------- 2 ---------------- | Traceback (most recent call last): | File "/home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_user_group_config_property.py", line 109, in test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly | assert actual_groups == expected_groups, ( | AssertionError: Group mismatch for user 'test-admin': | Missing groups: ['Regio_Pressmeet'] | Extra groups: [] | assert {'Communicati...ts_Read', ...} == {'Communicati...ts_Read', ...} | | Extra items in the right set: | 'Regio_Pressmeet' | | Full diff: | { | 'Communication_CRUD', | 'Communication_Export', | 'Communication_Read', | 'Events_CRUD', | 'Events_Export', | 'Events_Read', | 'Members_CRUD', | 'Members_Export', | 'Members_Read', | 'Members_Status_Approve', | 'Products_CRUD', | 'Products_Export', | 'Products_Read', | 'Regio_All', | - 'Regio_Pressmeet', | 'System_CRUD', | 'System_Logs_Read', | 'System_User_Management', | 'Webshop_Management', | 'hdcnLeden', | } | Falsifying example: test_user_groups_match_requirements_exactly( | self=<tests.unit.test_user_group_config_property.TestProperty5TestUserGroupConfigurationCorrectness object at 0x7f1670271990>, | user={'username': 'test-admin', | 'email': 'webmaster+testadmin@h-dcn.nl', | 'groups': ['Products_CRUD', | 'Products_Export', | 'Products_Read', | 'Webshop_Management', | 'Members_Read', | 'Members_CRUD', | 'Members_Export', | 'Members_Status_Approve', | 'System_CRUD', | 'System_Logs_Read', | 'System_User_Management', | 'Communication_CRUD', | 'Communication_Read', | 'Communication_Export', | 'Events_CRUD', | 'Events_Read', | 'Events_Export', | 'Regio_All', | 'hdcnLeden']}, | ) | username=test-admin | actual_groups=['Communication_CRUD', 'Communication_Export', 'Communication_Read', 'Events_CRUD', 'Events_Export', 'Events_Read', 'Members_CRUD', 'Members_Export', 'Members_Read', 'Members_Status_Approve', 'Products_CRUD', 'Products_Export', 'Products_Read', 'Regio_All', 'System_CRUD', 'System_Logs_Read', 'System_User_Management', 'Webshop_Management', 'hdcnLeden'] | expected_groups=['Communication_CRUD', 'Communication_Export', 'Communication_Read', 'Events_CRUD', 'Events_Export', 'Events_Read', 'Members_CRUD', 'Members_Export', 'Members_Read', 'Members_Status_Approve', 'Products_CRUD', 'Products_Export', 'Products_Read', 'Regio_All', 'Regio_Pressmeet', 'System_CRUD', 'System_Logs_Read', 'System_User_Management', 'Webshop_Management', 'hdcnLeden'] | Explanation: | These lines were always and only run by failing examples: | /home/runner/work/h-dcn/h-dcn/backend/tests/unit/test_user_group_config_property.py:113 | | You can reproduce this example by temporarily adding @reproduce_failure('6.155.2', b'AEEA') as a decorator on your test case +------------------------------------

testcase.name: test_all_configured_users_are_in_requirements

failure.message:
AssertionError: User 'test-event-participant' exists in seed config but is not defined in requirements assert 'test-event-participant' in {'test-admin': {'Communication_CRUD', 'Communication_Export', 'Communication_Read', 'Events_CRUD', 'Events_Export', 'Events_Read', ...}, 'test-lid': {'Regio_Pressmeet', 'club_test_presmeet', 'hdcnLeden'}, 'test-treasurer': {'Finance_CRUD', 'Finance_Read', 'hdcnLeden'}, 'test-presmeet': {'Regio_Pressmeet', 'hdcnLeden'}, ...} Falsifying example: test_all_configured_users_are_in_requirements( self=<tests.unit.test_user_group_config_property.TestProperty5TestUserGroupConfigurationCorrectness object at 0x7f1670270690>, user={'username': 'test-event-participant', 'email': 'pjageers+testpresmeet@gmail.com', 'groups': ['event_participant', 'hdcnLeden']}, ) username=test-event-participant
You can reproduce this example by temporarily adding @reproduce_failure('6.155.2', b'AEED') as a decorator on your test case

testcase.name: test_requirements_cover_all_seed_users
testcase.failure: tests/unit/test_user_group_config_property.py:146: in test_requirements_cover_all_seed_users assert seed_usernames == requirements_usernames, ( E AssertionError: User set mismatch: E In requirements but not in seed: ['test-presmeet'] E In seed but not in requirements: ['test-event-participant'] E assert {'test-admin'...st-treasurer'} == {'test-admin'...st-treasurer'} E E Extra items in the left set: E 'test-event-participant' E Extra items in the right set: E 'test-presmeet' E E Full diff: E { E 'test-admin', E + 'test-event-participant', E 'test-lid', E - 'test-presmeet', E 'test-readonly', E 'test-treasurer', E }
